title_en: 5. Would you like to meet him outside the institution? (cont.)
content_en: I recall that a prominent preacher who had been blessed with sound intellect and eloquence would frequently deliver lectures and sermons. Whether it was a Friday sermon, a Fatwa session, or a university lecture, he would continuously be speaking. Peo85 The art of listening ple would watch him on satellite television channels and always look forward to listening to his words. Except, that is, for his wife. Whenever he was with his wife, instead of listening to her story, he would be continuously speaking non-stop, as usual. She would often complain about him but he wouldn’t know why she would do so. Everyone would respect and praise him except for her. So, one day, he decided to take her with him to one of his lectures to appreciate what she had not heard before. He said to her, “Would you like to come with me?” She said, “To where?” He said, “To a lecture by a preacher. Perhaps we will beneﬁt from it.” She got into the car with him. When they arrived at their destination, they got out of the car and walked until they reached the door of the mosque. A huge crowd had turned up to listen to this excellent lecturer. She went into the women’s section whilst he walked through the crowd, sat on the chair and began his lecture. The people were listening to the lecture attentively, and even his wife was probably amazed at his performance. When the lectured ﬁnished, he went out to his car, pleased with his performance. His wife came and sat in the passenger seat, and as they sat in the car, he gave her no opportunity to speak, as usual, and began to talk about the crowds, the beauty of the mosque, etc. He then asked her, “What do you think of today’s lecture?” She said, “It was good and very effective. Who was the lec85 The art of listening turer?”’ He said, “Strange! You didn’t recognise the voice?” She said, “Well, with all the crowd and the poor loudspeakers, I couldn’t pay much attention.” He said, with all pride, “I was the lecturer!” She said, “Ah! I was thinking to myself all through the talk when will he ever stop!” Therefore, to listen attentively to others is a skill in and of itself. Some people forget that Allah has only given them one tongue while he has given them two ears - so that they may hear more than they speak. Some people, if given the choice to balance their speaking and hearing, would opt for having two ears and two tongues, due to their obsession with speaking! Train yourself to listen to others attentively. If you do have criticisms to make, then do not be hasty in making them. During the early days of Islam, the Muslims were few in number. The disbelievers would reject the Prophet  and discourage people from listening to him. They would spread rumours about him that he was a soothsayer, a liar, a madman, or even a magician. One day, a man called Dhuman came to Makkah. He was a wise man with knowledge of medicine who would also treat people with mental illness or problems with magic. When he began to mix with the people of Makkah, he heard some of the ignorants saying, “Then the madman came... We then saw the madman...” Dhumad said, “Where is this man? Perhaps Allah will cure him by my hands.” The art of listening The people directed him to the Messenger of Allah . When he saw the Prophet  and reﬂected upon his face, he found it to be bright. Dhumad nevertheless told the Prophet  the reason why he had come, “O Muhammad, I am an expert on incantations. Allah cures whoever He wills by my hands. Come to me and I will treat you.” The man began to speak about his treatments expertise as the Prophet  listened attentively. What was he listening to so attentively? He was listening to the words of a disbeliever who had come to treat his ‘mental illness’! And how wise he was! When Dhumad ﬁnished what he had to say, the Prophet  said very calmly, “All praise be to Allah, we praise Him and seek His help. Whoever Allah guides cannot be misled. And whoever He sets astray then there is none to guide him. I bear witness that there is no one worthy of worship except Allah alone, with no partner.” Dhumad trembled and said, “Repeat these words to me.” The Prophet  repeated the words. Dhumad said, “By Allah, I have heard the words of soothsayers, magicians, and poets, but I have never heard the like of these words, which reached the depths of the sea.
title_id: 5. Apakah Anda ingin bertemu dengannya di luar lembaga? (lanjutan)
content_id: Saya teringat bahwa seorang penceramah terkemuka yang dikaruniai akal yang sehat dan kefasihan seringkali menyampaikan ceramah dan khutbah. Baik itu khutbah Jumat, sesi fatwa, atau kuliah universitas, beliau terus-menerus berbicara. Orang-orang menontonnya di saluran televisi satelit dan selalu menantikan untuk mendengar kata-katanya. Kecuali, yaitu istrinya. Setiap kali dia bersama istrinya, alih-alih mendengarkan ceritanya, dia terus-menerus berbicara tanpa henti, seperti biasanya. Istrinya sering mengeluhkannya tetapi dia tidak mengerti mengapa dia melakukan itu. Semua orang menghormati dan memujinya kecuali istrinya. Maka suatu hari, dia memutuskan untuk mengajaknya ke salah satu ceramahnya agar dia bisa menghargai apa yang belum pernah dia dengar sebelumnya. Dia berkata kepadanya, "Apakah kamu ingin ikut bersamaku?" Dia berkata, "Ke mana?" Dia berkata, "Ke ceramah oleh seorang penceramah. Mungkin kita akan mendapat manfaat darinya." Dia naik ke mobil bersamanya. Ketika mereka tiba di tujuan, mereka turun dari mobil dan berjalan sampai mereka mencapai pintu masjid. Sebuah kerumunan besar telah hadir untuk mendengarkan penceramah yang luar biasa ini. Dia masuk ke bagian wanita sementara dia berjalan melalui kerumunan, duduk di kursi dan memulai ceramahnya. Orang-orang mendengarkan ceramah dengan penuh perhatian, dan bahkan istrinya pun mungkin takjub dengan penampilannya. Ketika ceramah selesai, dia keluar ke mobilnya, puas dengan penampilannya. Istrinya datang dan duduk di kursi penumpang, dan saat mereka duduk di mobil, dia tidak memberinya kesempatan untuk berbicara, seperti biasa, dan mulai berbicara tentang kerumunan, keindahan masjid, dan sebagainya. Dia kemudian bertanya kepadanya, "Bagaimana menurutmu ceramah hari ini?" Dia berkata, "Bagus dan sangat efektif. Siapa penceramahnya?" Dia berkata, "Aneh! Kamu tidak mengenali suaranya?" Dia berkata, "Yah, dengan semua kerumunan dan pengeras suara yang buruk, aku tidak bisa terlalu memperhatikan." Dia berkata dengan penuh kebanggaan, "Akulah penceramahnya!" Dia berkata, "Ah! Aku terus berpikir sepanjang pembicaraan kapan dia akan berhenti!" Oleh karena itu, mendengarkan orang lain dengan penuh perhatian adalah sebuah keterampilan tersendiri. Beberapa orang lupa bahwa Allah hanya memberi mereka satu lidah sementara Dia memberi mereka dua telinga - agar mereka bisa mendengar lebih banyak daripada yang mereka bicarakan. Beberapa orang, jika diberi pilihan untuk menyeimbangkan berbicara dan mendengarkan mereka, akan memilih memiliki dua telinga dan dua lidah, karena obsesi mereka terhadap berbicara! Latihlah dirimu untuk mendengarkan orang lain dengan penuh perhatian. Jika kamu memiliki kritikan untuk disampaikan, maka janganlah tergesa-gesa dalam menyampaikannya. Pada masa-masa awal Islam, kaum Muslim sedikit jumlahnya. Orang-orang kafir menolak Nabi dan mencegah orang-orang dari mendengarkannya. Mereka menyebarkan rumor tentangnya bahwa dia adalah dukun, pembohong, orang gila, atau bahkan penyihir. Suatu hari, seorang pria bernama Dhuman datang ke Makkah. Dia adalah seorang pria bijak dengan pengetahuan tentang kedokteran yang juga mengobati orang-orang dengan penyakit jiwa atau masalah sihir. Ketika dia mulai bergaul dengan orang-orang Makkah, dia mendengar beberapa orang bodoh berkata, "Kemudian orang gila itu datang... Kami kemudian melihat orang gila itu..." Dhumad berkata, "Di mana orang ini? Mungkin Allah akan menyembuhkannya melalui tanganku." Seni mendengarkan Orang-orang mengarahkannya kepada Rasulullah shallallahu alaihi wa sallam. Ketika dia melihat Nabi dan merenungkan wajahnya, dia mendapatinya cerah. Dhumad bagaimanapun memberitahu Nabi alasan mengapa dia datang, "Wahai Muhammad, aku adalah ahli dalam mantra. Allah menyembuhkan siapa pun yang Dia kehendaki melalui tanganku. Datanglah kepadaku dan aku akan mengobatimu." Pria itu mulai berbicara tentang keahlian pengobatannya sementara Nabi mendengarkan dengan penuh perhatian. Apa yang beliau dengarkan dengan begitu penuh perhatian? Beliau mendengarkan kata-kata seorang kafir yang datang untuk mengobati penyakit jiwa beliau! Dan betapa bijaknya beliau! Ketika Dhumad selesai dengan apa yang ingin dia katakan, Nabi dengan sangat tenang bersabda, "Segala puji bagi Allah, kami memuji-Nya dan memohon pertolongan-Nya. Barang siapa yang Allah tunjuki tidak bisa disesatkan. Dan barang siapa yang Dia sesatkan maka tidak ada yang bisa memberinya petunjuk. Aku bersaksi bahwa tidak ada yang berhak disembah kecuali Allah seorang diri, tanpa sekutu." Dhumad gemetar dan berkata, "Ulangi kata-kata ini kepadaku." Nabi mengulang kata-kata itu. Dhumad berkata, "Demi Allah, aku telah mendengar kata-kata dukun, penyihir, dan penyair, tetapi aku tidak pernah mendengar kata-kata seperti ini, yang sampai ke kedalaman lautan.
content_ar: يختتم المؤلف كتابه "استمتع بحياتك" بمزيد من المواقف والحكايات المُستقاة من السيرة النبوية الشريفة وسير الصحابة الكرام. ويؤكد أهمية حسن الخلق والتواضع والإخلاص في التعامل مع الناس، مُختتمًا بنصائح عملية لمن يريد أن يعيش حياةً سعيدة مع نفسه ومع مجتمعه.
